metadata.description: Stride task management skills for AI agents

plugins.name: stride
plugins.description: Task lifecycle skills for Stride kanban: claiming, completing, creating tasks and goals, enriching tasks, workflow orchestration, subagent dispatch, and automatic hook execution via Claude Code hooks. v1.24.0+: review reports must be delivered complete — NO EXCEPTIONS (goal G222). Every part of a dispatched review report is now mandatory on every task completion: all review information the task supplies (`security_considerations`, `testing_strategy`, `patterns_to_follow`, `pitfalls`, `acceptance_criteria`) must be passed to the reviewer, and the reviewer's entire structured output (every project check and every section verdict) must reach the server intact — not for small or trivial tasks, never with a promise to fix it later. Plugin-side source fix for the recurring defect where a task's security considerations came back `not_assessed` and the project-checks list was silently truncated (3 of 26 reached the server). `skills/stride-workflow/SKILL.md` (W1072) now passes every review field the task supplies to the reviewer (it previously listed only four, omitting `security_considerations`, so it came back `not_assessed`); `agents/task-reviewer.md` (W1073/W1076) reserves `not_assessed` strictly for a section the task itself left empty and single-sources its input contract; the `reviewer_result` passthrough (W1074, in `skills/stride-workflow/SKILL.md` + `skills/stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md`) is now a mechanical whole-object copy with a count-checked self-check (submitted `project_checks` count must equal the reviewer's); and `skills/stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md` (W1075) adds a mandatory pre-submission self-check hard gate that refuses to submit a thin or task-inconsistent report. Documentation/agent-prompt change only — no wire-shape, hook, `.stride.md`, or `.stride_auth.md` change; the Kanban server independently hard-rejects an incomplete or task-inconsistent report (goal G221), so the two halves enforce completeness from both ends. v1.23.0+: the `agents/task-reviewer.md` `project_checks[]` per-entry `status` enum gains a third value `not_applicable` (alongside `met`/`not_met`) and the reviewer must now emit one entry for EVERY top-level `CODE-REVIEW.md` bullet — bullets with no bearing on the diff are marked `not_applicable` with a one-line reason rather than omitted, so the Kanban review queue's Code review panel renders the full checklist instead of a partial one. `not_applicable` is approval-neutral (no paired `issues[]` entry, never triggers `changes_requested`); `schema_version` bumps `"1.3"` → `"1.4"`. Additive and forward-compatible — `reviewer_result` is `:jsonb` and persisted verbatim, so the new value flows through untouched; the review-queue "N/A" pill rendering ships kanban-side independently. v1.22.1+: fixes a data-loss defect where the completion flow dropped `reviewer_result.project_checks` — `skills/stride-workflow/SKILL.md` Step 6 built `reviewer_result` from an enumerated copy-list that omitted `project_checks`, so the reviewer's CODE-REVIEW.md audit was silently dropped and the Kanban review queue's Code review panel rendered nothing. The guidance is now a verbatim passthrough (copy the reviewer's entire parsed JSON, overlay only the legacy summary fields), and `agents/task-reviewer.md` gains a consumption invariant making it the single source of truth for the structured key-set so consumers never re-enumerate (with a `stride-completing-tasks` completion-checklist guard). Documentation/skill-instruction change only — no wire-shape change; `project_checks[]` already existed (v1.18.0+) and was already rendered. The kanban-repo regression test ships independently. Source: D63/W1049/W1051 (goal G218). v1.22.0+: the `after_doing` hook (`hooks/stride-hook.sh` + `hooks/stride-hook.ps1`) uploads the per-file diff snapshot to `/api/tasks/:id/changed_files` as a transport-encoded envelope — `{"changed_files":{"encoding":"base64","data":"<single-line-base64>"}}` — instead of the raw `{"changed_files":[...]}` array, so an edge request filter (WAF) in front of the Stride server cannot misread a dense code diff as an attack payload and silently drop the upload (which left `changed_files` empty in the review queue); the server decodes the base64 back to the identical list. Falls back to the raw array shape when `base64` is unavailable (the object wrapper is preserved on both paths so the value never lands at `params['_json']` as NULL), and a non-2xx upload response is now surfaced as a stderr warning instead of being discarded (non-fatal to completion; the bearer token is never logged). Requires the Kanban server to accept the `base64` / `gzip+base64` envelope on `/changed_files` (decoded with bounded streaming inflate + size guards), which ships independently in the kanban repo. Source: D61. v1.21.0+: elevates `security_considerations` to a first-class, review_queue-scored field at parity with `testing_strategy` — the three task-authoring skills (`stride-creating-tasks`, `stride-enriching-tasks`, `stride-creating-goals`) name it as the 5th scored field (callouts four → five) with array-of-strings shape, examples, and checklist/red-flag/rationalization rows; the `task-enricher` and `task-decomposer` agents derive and emit it; and `agents/task-reviewer.md` gains a **Security Considerations Alignment** review step plus a `security_considerations` `{ "status": "passed" | "failed" | "not_assessed" }` section-verdict object that confirms the considerations were actually implemented (input validation, authz boundaries, secret handling, injection surfaces, data exposure). The `issues[]` `category` enum gains `"security"`; the consistency rule and review-queue tile-reading list extend to it; `schema_version` bumps `"1.2"` → `"1.3"`; and `skills/stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md` + `skills/stride-workflow/SKILL.md` carry the verdict verbatim into `reviewer_result`. Wire-shape additive and forward-compatible — the Kanban server stores `reviewer_result` as `:jsonb` and tolerates the new key; reviewers on an older `schema_version` omit it. The Kanban-server UI half (rendering the security-considerations review result on the Review Queue) ships independently. v1.20.0+: adds two slash commands — `/stride:create-tasks` and `/stride:create-goals` — that create work tasks (or a goal with nested tasks) optionally seeded from a directory of project markdown via `--dir <path>` (alias `--context`), loaded as a read-only context bundle and routed through the `stride-workflow` orchestrator (never the creation sub-skills directly); `skills/stride-workflow/SKILL.md` documents the command entry points and the context-threading contract, and `stride-creating-tasks` / `stride-creating-goals` each gain a Consuming Provided Context section. v1.19.0+: the `agents/task-reviewer.md` agent emits three new per-section verdict objects — `testing_strategy` / `patterns` / `pitfalls`, each `{ "status": "passed" | "failed" | "not_assessed" }` — that the Kanban review queue renders as section tiles; a `"failed"` verdict must be backed by a matching-category `issues[]` entry (`testing` / `pattern` / `pitfall`), and vice-versa, so the tiles always agree with the issue list. `schema_version` bumps `"1.1"` → `"1.2"`. `skills/stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md` now persists the reviewer agent's full structured JSON block verbatim as `reviewer_result` (`status` / `issue_counts` / `issues[]` / `acceptance_criteria[]` / `project_checks[]` / the section verdicts) merged with the legacy summary fields (`dispatched` / `duration_ms` / `issues_found` / `acceptance_criteria_checked`), instead of the thin issues-found envelope that stripped the issues, acceptance verdicts, and code-review checks the review queue renders; extraction is delegated to the `stride-workflow` Step 6 "Extracting the structured review block", and the schema is owned by `agents/task-reviewer.md` (cited, not redefined). The `after_doing` hook (`hooks/stride-hook.sh` + `hooks/stride-hook.ps1`) now resolves the `/changed_files` upload URL and bearer token from `$PROJECT_DIR/.stride_auth.md` (the production `**API Token:**` line, never `**Local API Token:**`), falling back to the intercepted `$COMMAND` literals — fixing the empty `changed_files` that occurred on every completion whose curl used `$STRIDE_API_URL` / `$STRIDE_API_TOKEN` shell variables (fire-and-forget, non-fatal, token never logged; new hook tests cover the variable-command path). Forward-compatible additive change — older orchestrators emitting only the legacy envelope still validate, and the review queue derives the section tiles from `issues[]` categories when explicit section objects are absent. The Kanban-server UI half (review-queue section tiles, status pill, panel hardening) ships independently. v1.18.0+: adds a new step 6 **Project-Level Checks** to `agents/task-reviewer.md` — the reviewer reads `CODE-REVIEW.md` from the project root, parses each top-level Markdown bullet beneath any heading as a standing review check, and evaluates each against the diff with the same Met / Not Met semantics already used for `acceptance_criteria` (nested sub-bullets are NOT separate checks). A case-sensitive `CRITICAL:` prefix on a bullet maps to severity `critical`; default is `important` (the prefix is stripped from recorded text). Reviewer output gains a new top-level `project_checks[]` field in the structured JSON block — each entry has `check`, `source` (always the literal `"CODE-REVIEW.md"`), `status` (`"met"` | `"not_met"`), and `evidence`. Every `not_met` project_check MUST produce a paired `issues[]` entry with `category: "project_check"` and the derived severity, so per-severity telemetry (`issue_counts`) stays consistent with the per-check verdict. `schema_version` bumps from `"1.0"` to `"1.1"`; the `status` rule extends to `"changes_requested"` if any project_check is `not_met` (alongside the existing critical/important issue and not_met acceptance_criterion triggers); the `issues[]` `category` enum gains `"project_check"`. When `CODE-REVIEW.md` does not exist, the field renders as `project_checks: []` and behavior is byte-identical to 1.17.3. Forward-compatible additive change — older orchestrators parsing the prior `"1.0"` schema continue to read the new payload. Sibling reviewer-variant prompts (Cursor, Windsurf, Continue, Codex, Gemini) reference task-reviewer.md as the schema of record and are not modified. v1.17.3+: strengthens the three task-authoring SKILL.md files (stride-creating-tasks, stride-enriching-tasks, stride-creating-goals) to name the four review_queue-scored fields explicitly — `acceptance_criteria`, `testing_strategy`, `pitfalls`, `patterns_to_follow` — and the empty-pill consequence of omitting any of them. Each skill gains a top-of-file ⚠️ REVIEW QUEUE SCORING callout, plus reinforcement in existing Red Flags - STOP / Rationalization Table / Phase 4 checklist / Task Nesting Rules structures (no new top-level sections introduced). stride-enriching-tasks promotes the four fields to individual mandatory-for-review items in the Phase 4 16-item pre-submission checklist (replacing prior single-line bundling); stride-creating-goals stresses nested tasks are graded individually — no "it's just a subtask" discount. Content-only release: no hook script, parser, env-var matrix, API field shape, or workflow step changed. v1.17.0+: adds the `## after_goal` hook section — fires after the parent goal's final child task completes, blocking, 60s timeout, same single-bash-fence parsing rule as the four existing hooks. The executor forwards GOAL_ID/GOAL_IDENTIFIER/GOAL_TITLE/GOAL_DESCRIPTION env vars sourced verbatim from the server's hook.env (agent never invents or looks up these client-side). Missing `## after_goal` section parses as a clean no-op so older `.stride.md` files keep working; the grace-window server path bridges agent runtimes that don't speak the after_goal protocol. SKILL.md adds a five-row hooks reference table (timing/blocking/timeout columns), a Hook Environment Variables matrix, and a Canonical Hook Examples block — explicitly noting the hook is general-purpose (Slack notifications, artifact archival, release pipelines, project-level smoke tests are equally valid uses). Server-side companion (kanban-side): W498 emits `[:kanban, :api, :after_goal_delivered]` telemetry on each real delivery, W499 surfaces an after_goal adoption tile (distinct boards reporting in trailing 7d, grace-worker excluded), W500 surfaces p50/p95 goal-to-Done latency (14d window, server-side timestamps, grace-worker excluded). v1.16.0+: the `after_doing` hook now PUTs `.stride-changed-files.json` to `/api/tasks/:id/changed_files` immediately after writing it to disk, so agents no longer need to inline `--argjson cf` in the completion curl — the server-side endpoint (kanban W777, Stride server v1.16.0+) is the upload target; URL and Bearer token are extracted from the intercepted completion command, no `.stride_auth.md` read; fire-and-forget, no new env vars; PowerShell mirror in `hooks/stride-hook.ps1`; `stride-completing-tasks` SKILL.md drops the inline-cat from canonical examples and demotes it to a back-compat fallback section for older Stride deployments. v1.15.1+: the three canonical inline-cat occurrences in skills/stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md now use the defaulted form `"${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-.}/.stride-changed-files.json"` so the snapshot read falls back to the current working directory when `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` is unset/empty (the shape under Claude Code's TypeScript SDK) — agents on the SDK runtime stop POSTing `changed_files: []`. Wire shape unchanged; the inline structure and `|| echo '[]'` fallback are byte-preserved. v1.15.0+: `capture_changed_files()` reflects the agent's full working state at completion time, regardless of commit state — committed, staged, modified-uncommitted, AND untracked-new files all surface in the snapshot, with untracked text files appearing as synthesized new-file unified patches; the canonical API Request Format example in skills/stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md now inlines the snapshot read inside the curl invocation (via `jq -n --argjson cf "$(cat "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-.}/.stride-changed-files.json" 2>/dev/null || echo '[]')"`) so the read happens AFTER the PreToolUse-on-complete hook has written the file; the kanban-repo `docs/diff-contract.md` encoding guidance was updated in lockstep to document the working-tree-relative semantic. v1.14.0+: implements the G148/W719 per-file diff JSON contract — the after_doing hook captures changed files per the contract, truncates at exactly 500 lines with the contract marker, emits the binary placeholder for files git marks binary in --numstat, and writes the resulting JSON array to .stride-changed-files.json for the completion payload to carry as the optional `changed_files` field. v1.14.1+: the canonical API Request Format example body and the pre-completion verification checklist in skills/stride-completing-tasks/SKILL.md both surface `changed_files` directly, so agents reading the standard example are prompted to embed the snapshot during normal payload assembly. Reviewers see per-file unified-patch text inline in the Stride review queue. Backward-compatible — legacy completions without changed_files continue to validate. v1.13.0+: the task-reviewer agent emits a structured, parseable JSON review-report block alongside the prose summary, so downstream tooling can extract per-severity issue counts, per-issue file:line locations, and acceptance-criteria coverage.

plugins.name: stride-security-review
plugins.description: AI-powered security review via /stride-security-review:security-review. Two scan modes: diff (default, PR-gating) and --full (codebase posture check, batched with cross-batch dedup and skipped-files reporting). Detects 10 universal vulnerability classes plus 5 MAESTRO-derived agentic-AI classes (prompt injection, tool abuse, agent trust boundary, model output execution, vector-store poisoning). Seven framework rule packs (new in 2.3.0): Android/Kotlin (exported components, WebView JS bridge RCE, cleartext traffic, SharedPreferences credentials, SQLite injection, trust-all SSL), Django/Python (mark_safe, raw/extra SQL, CSRF, production settings, mass-assignment, open-redirect, deserialization, SSRF, DRF mass-assignment), Express/Node.js (reflected XSS, eval RCE, child_process injection, Mongoose NoSQL injection, prototype pollution), iOS/Swift (UserDefaults credentials, WKWebView file access, ATS arbitrary loads, URLSession trust-all, deep-link missing auth), Phoenix/Elixir (raw/1, force_ssl, CSRF, fragment interpolation, LiveView scope, mass-assignment, Token replay, System.cmd shell, LiveView upload guards, open-redirect), Rails/Ruby (html_safe, find_by_sql, CSRF, params.permit!, eval/send, open-redirect, deserialization, connection.execute, missing-auth, JSON-leak), React/Next.js (dangerouslySetInnerHTML, API route missing auth, redirect open-redirect, javascript: href scheme, getServerSideProps secret leak). Plus a multi-platform CI/CD pipeline pack (8 platforms) and a framework-agnostic Web defense-in-depth pack covering security headers (CSP/HSTS/X-Frame-Options/cookie flags). Every finding carries CWE-ID and OWASP Top 10 references. Opt-in flags: --maestro (7-layer threat classification with explicit non-AI mapping), --rci [N] (recursive criticism passes), --baseline (suppress acknowledged findings), --patches (surgical-fix unified diffs), --fail-on <severity> (CI exit-code gate), --base <ref> (PR-against-base diff scope), --sarif (SARIF v2.1.0 output for GitHub Code Scanning). Ships a reference GitHub Actions workflow and a TAP 13 eval runner over 64 fixtures with positive-and-negative-control coverage. Semantic analysis, not pattern matching. Loosely based on anthropics/claude-code-security-review.

plugins.name: stride-ideation
plugins.description: Turn a fuzzy idea into shipped Stride tasks via two slash commands. /stride-ideation:ideate drives an interactive round-based question loop (up to 4 batched AskUserQuestion per round, mandatory round-3 framing checkpoint, mandatory round-4 premortem in v0.3.0, seven hard-gated requirements sections with shape requirements on Assumptions — ranked highest-to-lowest risk with the riskiest marked — and Success Metrics — both leading and lagging indicators required — advisory requirements-reviewer pass) and writes a committed requirements markdown doc — the terminal state, never auto-funnels into the next step. New in v0.4.0: a --profile <lean|product|discovery> flag selects the round structure and reviewer rubric (lean = default, byte-for-byte v0.3.0; product = JTBD four-forces in Round 1 + optional Concrete Example section; discovery = Why-now + Alternative-options in Round 2). New in v0.5.0: a fourth --profile=lean-startup value adds a mandatory Round-5 MVP-design batch anchored on the (R)-marked Assumptions entry, an optional MVP / Validation experiment section in the doc, and two profile-aware advisory reviewer checks (MVP section presence, falsifiable success/failure criteria) — source techniques: Eric Ries (Lean Startup), Steve Blank (Customer Development), Giff Constable (Riskiest Assumption Test). /stride-ideation:stridify (new in v0.2.0; merged from the previous /decompose + /ship pair) takes the requirements doc and runs the full pipeline end-to-end: validates the seven sections, preflights auth from .stride_auth.md, dispatches the requirements-decomposer subagent (canonical batch shape embedded; two-reason multi-goal split rule of code-coupling plus ~10-task soft cap; cross-goal claim ordering documented in decomposition_notes), stamps source_spec + source_spec_sha256, writes and commits a sibling timestamped batch JSON for audit, then strips local-audit fields and POSTs to /api/tasks/batch with token hygiene (no curl -v, no token in logs or errors), rendering the created G/W identifiers. Replaces superpowers:brainstorming for Stride projects; the two skills coexist for non-Stride projects where brainstorming remains the right fit. New in v0.7.0: a four-layer resilience model on /stridify — (1) a bounded subagent-dispatch retry in Step 7 (3 attempts, ~30s/~90s backoff on HTTP 529 / transient network / 'overloaded' classifications; terminal classifications still fail fast), (2) a new --goal <name|index> flag that scopes the decomposer prompt to one surface from the doc's ## Decomposition seams section (accepts integer indices AND slug-matching strings; both --goal value and --goal=value parse forms; the seams section remains freeform and ungated), (3) a retry-exhaustion fallback (new Step 7.5) that writes the assembled prompt + verbatim last error + recovery README to a sibling *-decomposer-prompt.md file when 3 transient failures pile up (Stride API POST is NOT attempted in this branch), and (4) a one-line preflight advisory in Step 2 when a doc enumerates more than 3 surfaces under ## Decomposition seams and --goal is not set. The Stride API POST itself is still NOT retried — partial-batch idempotency is not guaranteed; the user remains the retry mechanism on a 4xx/5xx. Test coverage grows from 59 to 109 assertions (3 new suites: test-stridify-retry, test-stridify-per-goal, test-stridify-fallback). Back-compat is preserved on the happy path — v0.6.0 users see byte-identical behavior unless they invoke --goal or hit a transient subagent failure.

plugins.name: stride-lite
plugins.description: Lightweight companion plugin to Stride — produces Stride-shaped goal and task markdown documents on disk from a free-text prompt plus an optional requirements directory. No API calls, no kanban setup, no auth files. Just markdown. Useful when you want the Stride field discipline (acceptance criteria, key files, pitfalls, testing strategy, dependencies) without committing to the full Stride server workflow — the documents it produces can be reviewed, edited, or later submitted to a real Stride deployment by hand or by tooling, but this plugin never does so itself. Surface: three slash commands and one workflow skill. /stride-lite:create-goal decomposes a prompt into a goal directory with one goal.md plus 1–8 child taskN.md files; /stride-lite:create-task renders a single task markdown file; /stride-lite:init scaffolds a project-local .stride_lite.md with email plus three hook sections (## before_task, ## after_task, ## after_goal). The stride-lite-workflow skill is the file-based equivalent of the full Stride plugin's stride-workflow — it walks a goal directory through an eight-step task lifecycle (select → before_task → explore → implement → after_task → review → review-loop decision → completion + archive), dispatching the two read-only subagents at the right moments. Three subagents are shipped: stride-lite:create-decomposer (decomposes a free-text prompt into a goal + child tasks YAML, no API calls), stride-lite:task-explorer (read-only enrichment with codebase context — Read / Grep / Glob, no Bash, no WebFetch; appends ## Exploration Report), and stride-lite:task-reviewer (reviews changes against acceptance criteria, pitfalls, patterns, and testing strategy with narrowly-scoped read-only git Bash; appends ## Review Report with embedded reviewer_result JSON, schema 1.1). v0.9.0+: hooks/hooks.json registers Claude Code PreToolUse/PostToolUse handlers that auto-fire the three .stride_lite.md sections at the corresponding workflow intercepts — ## before_task before the task-explorer Agent dispatch (blocking, exit 2 stops the dispatch), ## after_task before the task-reviewer Agent dispatch (blocking, exit 2 stops the dispatch), ## after_goal after the goal.md Edit/Write that appends ## Completion Summary (advisory — PostToolUse cannot roll back the write). Cross-platform from day one: hooks/stride-lite-hook.sh (POSIX bash, pure-bash JSON parsing, no jq dependency) and hooks/stride-lite-hook.ps1 (PowerShell 5.1+, ConvertFrom-Json / ConvertTo-Json, no module installs); the .sh script auto-delegates to .ps1 on native Windows (OSTYPE unset + COMSPEC set). v0.10.0+: stride-lite-workflow Step 8 final-task branch performs a terminal PENDING → IMPLEMENTED archive move — after ## after_goal fires, the goal directory moves from docs/implementation/PENDING/<slug>/ to docs/implementation/IMPLEMENTED/<slug>/ via git mv when tracked (preserves rename history) or plain mv otherwise. Collision suffixing on the target with -2 / -3 / … up to a 1000-iteration safety cap (never overwrites), after-goal-failure no-move guard (a structured failed-status JSON from the harness skips the move so the user can re-trigger), non-/PENDING/ path skip with a stderr warning (custom --output-dir is left where it is), and clean exit on filesystem-mv failure (logged, the workflow does not fail because of an archive-move failure). Single-task files under PENDING/tasks/ are NEVER moved — only goal directories.
